subject: Project Management
title: Agile's Lasting Legacy
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Agile's Lasting Legacy.

hook.text: Before Agile, customers saw software only at the end. After Agile, they saw it every two weeks.

buildup.text: Before Agile, change was the enemy. After Agile, change was expected and welcomed.

discovery.text: Agile shifted power from process to people — trusting teams to figure out how to deliver.

twist.text: The manifesto is just 68 words. Its influence on how humans collaborate is immeasurable.

climax.text: Agile didn't just change software. It changed how we think about work itself.

punchline.text: Sixty-eight words reshaped how the world works.

quiz.question: What is Agile's most fundamental shift in project thinking?
options: Trusting teams and welcoming change instead of fighting it
